Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Fleet managers, logistics coordinators, transportation supervisors, shipping operations staff, aviation ground crew, automotive fleet operators, supply chain managers, warehouse managers, delivery drivers, pilots, maritime officers
Team or Dept this is for: Transportation, Logistics, Operations, Fleet Management, Supply Chain, Shipping, Aviation, Maritime Operations, Automotive
Description: How spoofing can mislead AI navigation systems.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with how GPS and navigation systems are used in everyday transport, such as in vehicles, aircraft, or shipping vessels, though no technical or engineering background is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key methods used to spoof GPS and sensor data, and explain how these techniques can mislead AI-driven navigation systems across aviation, maritime, and automotive contexts. You will also be able to apply practical awareness strategies to recognise the warning signs of sensor manipulation and assess the potential risks posed to transportation and logistics operations.
